title: Neo NERV or WILLE whose side are you on
display_title: Neo NERV or WILLE whose side are you on
wikitext:
[]: Neo-NERV or WILLE--whose side are you on?             Quote Post by El Squibbonator » Fri Apr 25, 2014 7:20 pm As I'm sure we're all aware, the third movie shuffled the decks with regards to the characters and their allegiances. We now have a new group--WILLE--consisting of Misato, Ritsuko, Mari, Asuka, and most of the other old NERV employees. NERV, meanwhile, is still under the command of Gendo Ikari. Shinji, of course, seems to have no particular affiliation toward either group and in fact seems to despise both of them. The question is, then, when the inevitable final battle in 4.0 comes, which one will you be cheering? NERV or WILLE?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Apr 25, 2014 7:39 pm It seems little of a possibility to be on Gendo's and Fuyu's side (Kaworu wants to doublecross them as does Shinji once he finds out what they've been doing, and Rei Q doesn't have any real affilation, she just does what she's told, as evidenced by the ease with which she helped/followed Asuka once she found the slightest bit of motivation of her own, she was pretty much a meat automaton/ philosophical zombie when Shinji first met her) WILLE have sure compromized their ethics and you could see them in various lights, but at worst it's a black vs gray situation. The world will still be distinctly better off if they win than if NERV did. Despite the dehumanizing treatment Shinji gets, look at what the average WILLE members seems to experience in the organization - they're all very loyal to Misato and have a huge theme of hope/actually doing something/will to live goin on. You could discuss them all day, but it's pretty clear that SEELE or Gendo's victory is not much to root for. That said, I think the contrast between the two places is one of the things that are very well done in this movie, you have WILLE with its lively young bunch of newness and individuality/ragtag bunch of misfits (Really, look at Sakura, Hideki or Midori. They're innocents. They're the plucky new generation that will inherit the world. ) they all proudly wear their blue emblems and are very alive. Heck, even Asuka and Misato who have gotten old and bitter are still portrayed as very brave, strong and cool. Then you have neo nerv full of emptiness and decay and people who have long lost their humanity if they ever had them, it is all almost completely mechanized/automatic, the only real human there is Fuyutsuki, and he's old and bitter. Kaworu is probably a source of warmth and light, but he's "abstract"/"ethereal" enough not to create an idea of "being amongs people". It strongly evokes a ghost town, and the deeply sad thing is that Shinji belongs to the ghosts. He's not the young person that needs to survive anymore, he's a remnant of a dead world, of a generation that left Sakura, Midoru & co a way crappier world than the one they received, and Shinji is part of tha, albeit accidentally. He never wanted it, but it's still a horrible truth he can't escape. Midori was probably a toddler pre-timeskip, Sakura was an elementary school girl.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Apr 25, 2014 7:39 pm TEAM SHIN-CHAN 4EVA Fuck the rest. Except Mari. She's cool.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Fri Apr 25, 2014 7:44 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: TEAM SHIN-CHAN 4EVA

[]:             Quote Post by Firetrout » Fri Apr 25, 2014 7:47 pm Hmmmm...On one hand, we have two misguided elderly men that nearly succeeded in committing omnicide, twice. On the other hand we have everyone else who is trying to prevent them from succeeding, not to mention containing most of the NTE cast. That's pretty one-sided. NERV all the way.

[]:             Quote Post by heavytread » Fri Apr 25, 2014 8:08 pm What ever side Mari and Asuka are on. Fuck alliances to anyone else!

[]:             Quote Post by hui43210 » Fri Apr 25, 2014 8:38 pm Team WILLE all way fools!

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Apr 25, 2014 10:02 pm Neither- I'm team Evangelion pilots all the way. Until Wille or Nerv start being nicer, I'm not rooting for either one of them... but in a bind, I'd go for Wille.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Fri Apr 25, 2014 10:27 pm Kendrix wrote: It strongly evokes a ghost town, and the deeply sad thing is that Shinji belongs to the ghosts. He's not the young person that needs to survive anymore, he's a remnant of a dead world, of a generation that left Sakura, Midoru & co a way crappier world than the one they received, and Shinji is part of tha, albeit accidentally. He never wanted it, but it's still a horrible truth he can't escape. Midori was probably a toddler pre-timeskip, Sakura was an elementary school girl.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Apr 25, 2014 10:31 pm I didn't mean to start a discussion, just discuss the use of aesthetics with the factions. -.-

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Fri Apr 25, 2014 10:32 pm Kendrix wrote: I didn't mean to start a discussion, just discuss the use of aesthetics with the factions. -.-

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Apr 25, 2014 10:35 pm Okay, okay, I apologize! Anyways, it would obviously be nice if they all (or, being realistic, everyone appart from Gendo and Fuyu) just worked together and kicked some ass. One has to remember that we don't get to see WILLE from the easiest perspective. For the average joe, it's probably a cool thing to be part of - in any case, it seems to have been important to Anno to... hm. He bothered with all the new WILLE bridge bunnies many of which were all cute and/or sympathetic. The saddest thing is that most of them seem like they'd get along well with Shinji if they had met under less complicated circumstances. (Then again, any organization could look extremely awesomer if it had Sakura to put band-aids on the recruits) Sakura is definitely a huge asset. XD

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Fri Apr 25, 2014 10:44 pm I'm on whatever side of the fence Shinji is on, hopefully he is floating above them, and while they are are shouting up to him to save them from their miserable lives, he'll look down upon them and whisper "No" much like Asuka did to him but his will be much more meaningful and thoughtful.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Apr 25, 2014 11:14 pm Moved to proper section in Rebuild subforum. My gut instinct is to reply "Team Wille" (since they're doing their best to save the world, moral shades of gray aside), but for the time being I'll say "Team Eva Pilots" since they're the ones I really want to see become better people despite all the horrible things they've endured.

[]:             Quote Post by xyz346 » Fri Apr 25, 2014 11:30 pm Have to say Team Shinji at the moment.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Sat Apr 26, 2014 12:45 am Team Pen-Pen. ....I have to represent, yo.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Sat Apr 26, 2014 1:14 am Gob Hobblin wrote: Team Pen-Pen. ....I have to represent, yo.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Sat Apr 26, 2014 1:25 am A better ship! With blackjack, and hookers. In fact, forget the ship!

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sat Apr 26, 2014 1:27 am Are you... shipping? 'Cuz canon sinks ships, you know.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Sat Apr 26, 2014 1:34 am So NERV or WILLE, huh? Are those seriously my only choices? For me, the answer is quite obviously neither freakin one. Honestly, I hope they both fry in the most humiliating manner possible. Gendo has been and always will be a completely irredeemable prick and I've set aside a special bottle of champagne to celebrate the day when he receives the beating he so richly deserves. Meanwhile, WILLE seems to exist for the sole purpose of vilifying Shinji's actions at the end of 2.22, where he supposedly caused all the devastation viewed in 3.0 by defeating Zeruel (conveniently ignoring that Units 00 and 02 were getting their asses kicked, the vaunted dummy system was utterly incapable of activating Unit-01 and some type of berserker mode was ultimately required to finish the job, since nobody thought it was necessary to actually attach Unit-01's umbilical connection). In fact WILLE is so righteously indignant toward Shinji, so completely certain that he caused all of the world's problems, so absolutely terrified that he'll initiate another Impact if he ever attemps to pilot again, that they make the only rational choice left and install Unit-01 (the one thing IIRC which was previously exiled into orbit due to its irritating propensity of destroying the world at precisely the wrong moment) as the Wunder's power source. I mean, what could possibly go wrong? As far as I'm concerned, Misato and the rest of WILLE are just begging to be made an example of. Kendrix wrote: ...you have WILLE with its lively young bunch of newness and individuality/ragtag bunch of misfits (Really, look at Sakura, Hideki or Midori. They're innocents...
